Redesigned so X-Openai-Session feeds a new mcp_session CallerAnchor — the same anchor mechanism as pty_session/harness_session. A caller is registered once at initialize via ensure_mcp_session, then resolve_session_inner finds it read-only like any other anchor. Verified live over the daemon socket: register → who → channels_join → chat_write all resolve the same session with no special-case code. 941/941 tests pass, committed, redeployed with --oauth.

An MCP caller like ChatGPT now gets a real tenex-edge identity the same way a hosted CLI session does: X-Openai-Session is used as the stable key, initialize triggers one-time registration via ensure_mcp_session which mints a keypair through the same derivation as hosted sessions, and then every existing tool handler resolves the caller through the shared mcp_session anchor with no modifications. Verified end-to-end by hand over the daemon socket but not yet exercised from the real chatgpt.com UI.
